name: Lü Yi (Jiyang)
page_title: Lü Yi (Jiyang) 呂乂 (季陽) [Shu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Lü Yi, styled Jiyang, was the orphaned son of Lü Chang. Around AD 220 he was appointed an assistant officer of the Shu-Han salt monopoly under Wang Lian. He went on to hold high office in the state. (1)

1) Sanguozhi: Shu 9.

sections.short-biography: Served Shu as Grand Administrator of Hanzhong during Zhuge Liang’s time.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	呂乂 (季陽)


	 Simplified Chinese 
	吕乂 (季阳)


	 Pronunciation 
	Lv3 Yi4 (Ji4yang2)


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Lǚ Yì (Jìyáng)


	 Wade-Giles 
	Lü I (Chi-yang)

	 Birthplace 
	Nanyang commandery, possibly Bowang county

	 Name Notes 
	Some Brewitt-Taylor translations incorrectly list Lü Yi as ‘Lu Yin’. Some books, historical or simplified, including some Chinese Moss Roberts translations, list Lü Yi’s name as 呂義 or its simplified variant 呂义. They are presented as the same person because the two names are both used in reference to the same person, a Lü Yi who was Grand Administrator of Hanzhong during Zhuge Liang’s time, and because there is no reference to another Lü Yi in Shu.

full_text:
Lü Yi (Jiyang) 呂乂 (季陽)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Lü Yi (呂乂), styledJiyang (季陽), was born in Nanyang commandery, possibly Bowang county. Served Shu as Grand Administrator of Hanzhong during Zhuge Liang’s time. Lü Yi was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom. Lü Yi lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.
